mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of instances when unemployment crosses the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and economic momentum, driving sustained positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and slowing output growth, which compresses corporate profit margins and depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle periods, with a hit rate below 40 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates falling below 1.0 percent signal aggressive monetary accommodation that rapidly lowers discount rates and fuels short-term equity buying pressure.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations when the policy rate breaches the 1.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates labor market deterioration and weakening wage growth, leading to prolonged negative equity performance.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising unemployment and flat payrolls yields positive 60-day returns in over 50 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and improves risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth rates fail to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, showing a rank information coefficient below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surging above 105 during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and strong manufacturing demand, triggering immediate positive equity repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases when industrial production crosses the 105 threshold during recovery periods.
